chief_complaint: A 75-year-old male presented with a 2-day history of confusion, disorientation, and agitation.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and type 2 diabetes mellitus. He was on metformin, atorvastatin, and lisinopril. He had no history of smoking or alcohol use. He was admitted to the hospital 2 days ago for a urinary tract infection and was treated with antibiotics.
exam: Vital signs: blood pressure 160/90 mmHg, pulse 110 bpm, respiratory rate 24 breaths/min, and oxygen saturation 92% on room air. Physical examination revealed a disheveled and confused patient with a Glasgow Coma Scale of 12/15.
labs: ECG: new ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. hs-TnT: 120 ng/L (normal < 14 ng/L). Troponin I: 10 ng/L (normal < 0.4 ng/L). Chest X-ray: no signs of pulmonary edema.
